questions.questionHtml: Set of real values of k if the equation x2-k-1x+k2=0 has exactly one root in 1,2 is
questions.solutionText: Solution Exactly one root of quadratic equation x 2 - k - 1 x + k 2 = 0 , lies between 1 and 2 . Condition for exactly one root of quadratic equation f x = a x 2 + b x + c = 0 lies in the interval d , e will be f d · f e < 0 . ⇒ f 1 · f 2 < 0 ⇒ 1 - k - 1 + k 2 4 - k - 1 2 + k 2 < 0 ⇒ k 2 - k + 2 k 2 - 2 k + 6 < 0 For quadratic k 2 - k + 2 Discriminant, D = - 1 2 - 4 2 = - 7 . Since, the leading coefficient = 1 is positive and discriminant is negative, this implies it does not have real roots and will always be positive. For quadratic k 2 - 2 k + 6 , Discriminant, D = - 2 2 - 4 6 = - 20 Since, leading coefficient = 1 is positive and discriminant is negative, this implies it does not have real roots and will always be positive. Both quadratic expressions are always positive, so their product can not be negative. Hence, k ∈ ϕ .

⇒ f1·f2<0

⇒1-k-1+k24-k-12+k2<0

⇒k2-k+2k2-2k+6<0

For quadratic k2-k+2

Discriminant, D=-12-42=-7.

Since, the leading coefficient =1 is positive and discriminant is negative, this implies it does not have real roots and will always be positive.

For quadratic k2-2k+6,

questions.text: A manufacturing firm produces two products, A and B , using two machines. To produce one unit of A, 2 hours are required on Machine I and 1 hour on Machine II. To produce one unit of B, 1 hour is required on Machine I and 2 hours on Machine II. The maximum available time per day for Machine I is 2 0 hours and for Machine II is 1 5 hours. If the profit on one unit of A is ₹300 and on one unit of B is ₹500, what is the maximum daily profit the firm can achieve?

questions.html:
A manufacturing firm produces two products, A and B , using two machines. To produce one unit of A, 2 hours are required on Machine I and 1 hour on Machine II. To produce one unit of B, 1 hour is required on Machine I and 2 hours on Machine II.

The maximum available time per day for Machine I is 20 hours and for Machine II is 15 hours. If the profit on one unit of A is ₹300 and on one unit of B is ₹500, what is the maximum daily profit the firm can achieve?
questions.questionHtml:
A manufacturing firm produces two products, A and B , using two machines. To produce one unit of A, 2 hours are required on Machine I and 1 hour on Machine II. To produce one unit of B, 1 hour is required on Machine I and 2 hours on Machine II.

The maximum available time per day for Machine I is 20 hours and for Machine II is 15 hours. If the profit on one unit of A is ₹300 and on one unit of B is ₹500, what is the maximum daily profit the firm can achieve?
questions.solutionText: Solution Let x = units of A, y = units of B produced per day. Constraints: Machine I: 2 x + y ≤ 20 Machine II: x + 2 y ≤ 15 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 Objective: Maximise Z = 300 x + 500 y Corner points from constraint intersections: (0, 0): Z = 0 (10, 0): Z = 300 ( 10 ) + 0 = 3000 (0, 7.5): Z = 0 + 500 ( 7.5 ) = 3750 Intersection of 2 x + y = 20 and x + 2 y = 15 : From first: y = 20 − 2 x Sub into second: x + 2 ( 20 − 2 x ) = 15 x + 40 − 4 x = 15 ⇒ − 3 x = − 25 ⇒ x = 25 3 y = 20 − 50 3 = 10 3 Z = 300 × 25 3 + 500 × 10 3 = 2500 + 5000 3 = 7500 + 5000 3 = 12500 3 ≈ 4167 Comparing all corner points: maximum is at ( 25 3 , 10 3 ) giving Z = R s . 4167 Correct option: B) Rs. 4,167
questions.solution: Solution Let x = units of A, y = units of B produced per day. Constraints: Machine I: 2 x + y ≤ 20 Machine II: x + 2 y ≤ 15 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 Objective: Maximise Z = 300 x + 500 y Corner points from constraint intersections: (0, 0): Z = 0 (10, 0): Z = 300 ( 10 ) + 0 = 3000 (0, 7.5): Z = 0 + 500 ( 7.5 ) = 3750 Intersection of 2 x + y = 20 and x + 2 y = 15 : From first: y = 20 − 2 x Sub into second: x + 2 ( 20 − 2 x ) = 15 x + 40 − 4 x = 15 ⇒ − 3 x = − 25 ⇒ x = 25 3 y = 20 − 50 3 = 10 3 Z = 300 × 25 3 + 500 × 10 3 = 2500 + 5000 3 = 7500 + 5000 3 = 12500 3 ≈ 4167 Comparing all corner points: maximum is at ( 25 3 , 10 3 ) giving Z = R s . 4167 Correct option: B) Rs. 4,167
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let x = units of A, y = units of B produced per day.

Constraints:
Machine I: 2x+y≤20
Machine II: x+2y≤15
x≥0, y≥0

Objective: Maximise Z=300x+500y

Corner points from constraint intersections:

(0, 0): Z=0
(10, 0): Z=300(10)+0=3000
(0, 7.5): Z=0+500(7.5)=3750

Intersection of 2x+y=20 and x+2y=15:
From first: y=20−2x
Sub into second: x+2(20−2x)=15
x+40−4x=15⇒−3x=−25⇒x=253
y=20−503=103

Z=300×253+500×103=2500+50003=7500+50003=125003≈4167

Comparing all corner points: maximum is at (253, 103) giving Z=Rs. 4167

Correct option: B) Rs. 4,167

questions.solutionText: Solution We know that Mean = n p = 4 variance = n p q = 4 3 ⇒ n p q n p = 4 3 4 ⇒ q = 1 3 ⇒ p = 1 - 1 3 = 2 3 So, n p = 4 ⇒ n 2 3 = 4 ⇒ n = 6 Now, P X = 2 = C 2 6 p 2 q 4 ⇒ P X = 2 = 6 ! 2 ! × 4 ! 2 3 2 1 3 4 ⇒ P X = 2 = 60 3 6 ⇒ P X = 2 = 20 243

np=4

⇒PX=2=20243

questions.solutionText: Solution Value of determinant = Δ = α ( β ⋅ γ − α 2 ) − β ( α ⋅ γ − β 2 ) + γ ( α β − γ 2 ) → Δ = 3 ⋅ α ⋅ β ⋅ γ − ( α 3 + β 3 + γ 3 ) → α ⋅ β ⋅ γ = − b → α + β + γ = − a → α β + β γ + γ α = 0 And α 3 + β 3 + γ 3 − 3 α β γ = ( α + β + γ ) ( α 2 + β 2 + γ 2 − α β − β γ − γ α ) Also α 2 + β 2 + γ 2 = ( α + β + γ ) 2 − 2 ( α β + β γ + γ α ) Substituting we get; α 3 + β 3 + γ 3 = 3 ( − b ) + ( − a ) ( a 2 ) ∴ Δ = a 3 + 3 b − 3 b ⇒ Δ = a 3
questions.solution: Solution Value of determinant = Δ = α ( β ⋅ γ − α 2 ) − β ( α ⋅ γ − β 2 ) + γ ( α β − γ 2 ) → Δ = 3 ⋅ α ⋅ β ⋅ γ − ( α 3 + β 3 + γ 3 ) → α ⋅ β ⋅ γ = − b → α + β + γ = − a → α β + β γ + γ α = 0 And α 3 + β 3 + γ 3 − 3 α β γ = ( α + β + γ ) ( α 2 + β 2 + γ 2 − α β − β γ − γ α ) Also α 2 + β 2 + γ 2 = ( α + β + γ ) 2 − 2 ( α β + β γ + γ α ) Substituting we get; α 3 + β 3 + γ 3 = 3 ( − b ) + ( − a ) ( a 2 ) ∴ Δ = a 3 + 3 b − 3 b ⇒ Δ = a 3
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Value of determinant =Δ=α(β⋅γ−α2)−β(α⋅γ−β2)+γ(αβ−γ2)



→Δ=3⋅α⋅β⋅γ−(α3+β3+γ3)



→α⋅β⋅γ=−b



→α+β+γ=−a



→αβ+βγ+γα=0



And α3+β3+γ3−3αβγ=(α+β+γ)(α2+β2+γ2−αβ−βγ−γα)



Also α2+β2+γ2=(α+β+γ)2−2(αβ+βγ+γα)



Substituting we get;



α3+β3+γ3=3(−b)+(−a)(a2)



∴Δ=a3+3 b−3 b



⇒Δ=a3

questions.solutionText: Solution Given, P A ∪ B ¯ = 1 6 ⇒ 1 - P A ∪ B = 1 6 ⇒ 1 - P A - P B + P A ∩ B = 1 6 ⇒ P ( A ¯ ) - P B + P A ∩ B = 1 6 ⇒ 1 4 - P B + 1 4 = 1 6 ⇒ P B = 1 3 and P A = 1 - 1 4 = 3 4 As, P A ∩ B = P A P B So, events A and B are independent events but they are not equally likely.
questions.solution: Solution Given, P A ∪ B ¯ = 1 6 ⇒ 1 - P A ∪ B = 1 6 ⇒ 1 - P A - P B + P A ∩ B = 1 6 ⇒ P ( A ¯ ) - P B + P A ∩ B = 1 6 ⇒ 1 4 - P B + 1 4 = 1 6 ⇒ P B = 1 3 and P A = 1 - 1 4 = 3 4 As, P A ∩ B = P A P B So, events A and B are independent events but they are not equally likely.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given, PA∪ B¯=16⇒1-PA∪B=16

⇒1-PA-PB+PA∩B=16

⇒P(A¯)-PB+PA∩B=16

⇒14-PB+14=16

⇒PB=13 and PA=1-14=34

As, PA∩B=PAPB

So, events A and B are independent events but they are not equally likely.

questions.solutionText: Solution f ( x ) = x p sin ⁡ 1 x , x ≠ 0 and f ( x ) = 0 , x = 0 Since at x = 0 , f ( x ) is a continuous function ∴ lim x → 0 f ( x ) = f ( 0 ) = 0 ⇒ lim x → 0 x p sin ⁡ 1 x = 0 ⇒ p > 0 . f ( x ) is differentiable at x = 0 , if lim x → 0 f ( x ) − f ( 0 ) x − 0 exists ⇒ lim x → 0 x p sin ⁡ 1 x − 0 x − 0 exists ⇒ lim x → 0 x p − 1 sin ⁡ 1 x exists ⇒ p − 1 > 0 or p > 1 If p ≤ 1 , then lim x → 0 x p − 1 sin ⁡ ( 1 x ) does not exist and at x = 0 f ( x ) is not differentiable. ∴ for 0 < p ≤ 1 f ( x ) is a continuous function at x = 0 but not differentiable.
questions.solution: Solution f ( x ) = x p sin ⁡ 1 x , x ≠ 0 and f ( x ) = 0 , x = 0 Since at x = 0 , f ( x ) is a continuous function ∴ lim x → 0 f ( x ) = f ( 0 ) = 0 ⇒ lim x → 0 x p sin ⁡ 1 x = 0 ⇒ p > 0 . f ( x ) is differentiable at x = 0 , if lim x → 0 f ( x ) − f ( 0 ) x − 0 exists ⇒ lim x → 0 x p sin ⁡ 1 x − 0 x − 0 exists ⇒ lim x → 0 x p − 1 sin ⁡ 1 x exists ⇒ p − 1 > 0 or p > 1 If p ≤ 1 , then lim x → 0 x p − 1 sin ⁡ ( 1 x ) does not exist and at x = 0 f ( x ) is not differentiable. ∴ for 0 < p ≤ 1 f ( x ) is a continuous function at x = 0 but not differentiable.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
f(x)=xpsin⁡1x,x≠0 and f(x)=0,x=0
Since at x=0,f(x) is a continuous function
∴limx→0f(x)=f(0)=0⇒limx→0xpsin⁡1x=0⇒p>0.
f(x) is differentiable at x=0, if limx→0f(x)−f(0)x−0 exists
⇒limx→0xpsin⁡1x−0x−0 exists
⇒limx→0xp−1sin⁡1x exists ⇒p−1>0 or p>1
If p≤1, then limx→0xp−1sin⁡(1x) does not exist and at x=0f(x) is not differentiable.
∴ for 0<p≤1f(x) is a continuous function at x=0 but not differentiable.

questions.solutionText: Solution Since, α and β are the roots of 4 x 2 - 16 x + λ = 0 , λ ∈ R such that 1 < α < 2 and 2 < β < 3 . Therefore, we have Clearly, f 1 > 0 , f 2 < 0 , f 3 > 0 f 1 = - 12 + λ > 0 ⇒ λ > 12 f 2 = - 16 + λ < 0 ⇒ λ < 16 f 3 = - 12 + λ > 0 And for x ∈ R ⇒ D > 0 ⇒ 16 2 - 16 λ > 0 ⇒ 16 16 - λ > 0 ∴ 12 < λ < 16 ∴ λ ∈ 13 , 14 , 15 . Hence, three integral solutions.
questions.solution: Solution Since, α and β are the roots of 4 x 2 - 16 x + λ = 0 , λ ∈ R such that 1 < α < 2 and 2 < β < 3 . Therefore, we have Clearly, f 1 > 0 , f 2 < 0 , f 3 > 0 f 1 = - 12 + λ > 0 ⇒ λ > 12 f 2 = - 16 + λ < 0 ⇒ λ < 16 f 3 = - 12 + λ > 0 And for x ∈ R ⇒ D > 0 ⇒ 16 2 - 16 λ > 0 ⇒ 16 16 - λ > 0 ∴ 12 < λ < 16 ∴ λ ∈ 13 , 14 , 15 . Hence, three integral solutions.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Since, α and β are the roots of 4x2-16x+λ=0, λ∈R such that 1<α<2 and 2<β<3.
Therefore, we have

Clearly,
f1>0, f2<0, f3>0
f1=-12+λ>0⇒λ>12
f2=-16+λ<0⇒λ<16
f3=-12+λ>0
And for x∈R⇒D>0
⇒162-16λ>0
⇒1616-λ>0
∴ 12<λ<16
∴ λ∈13,14,15.
Hence, three integral solutions.

questions.solutionText: Solution Coefficient of x 10 in the sum = ( 15 10 ) + ( 16 10 ) + ⋯ + ( 30 10 ) By hockey stick identity: ∑ r = k n ( r k ) = ( n + 1 k + 1 ) ∑ r = 15 30 ( r 10 ) = ∑ r = 10 30 ( r 10 ) − ∑ r = 10 14 ( r 10 ) = ( 31 11 ) − ( 15 11 ) Correct option: (2) 31 C 11 − 15 C 11

Solution
Coefficient of x10 in the sum =(1510)+(1610)+⋯+(3010)

By hockey stick identity: ∑r=kn(rk)=(n+1k+1)

∑r=1530(r10)=∑r=1030(r10)−∑r=1014(r10)=(3111)−(1511)

Correct option: (2) 31C11−15C11

questions.questionText: If a = cos ⁡ α + i sin ⁡ α , b = cos ⁡ β + i sin ⁡ β , c = cos ⁡ γ + i sin ⁡ γ and b c + c a + a b = 1 , then cos ⁡ ( β − γ ) + cos ⁡ ( γ − α ) + cos ⁡ ( α − β ) is equal to
questions.question: If a = cos ⁡ α + i sin ⁡ α , b = cos ⁡ β + i sin ⁡ β , c = cos ⁡ γ + i sin ⁡ γ and b c + c a + a b = 1 , then cos ⁡ ( β − γ ) + cos ⁡ ( γ − α ) + cos ⁡ ( α − β ) is equal to
questions.prompt: If a = cos ⁡ α + i sin ⁡ α , b = cos ⁡ β + i sin ⁡ β , c = cos ⁡ γ + i sin ⁡ γ and b c + c a + a b = 1 , then cos ⁡ ( β − γ ) + cos ⁡ ( γ − α ) + cos ⁡ ( α − β ) is equal to
questions.html: If a=cos⁡α+isin⁡α,b=cos⁡β+isin⁡β, c=cos⁡γ+isin⁡γ and bc+ca+ab=1, then cos⁡(β−γ)+cos⁡(γ−α)+cos⁡(α−β) is equal to
questions.questionHtml: If a=cos⁡α+isin⁡α,b=cos⁡β+isin⁡β, c=cos⁡γ+isin⁡γ and bc+ca+ab=1, then cos⁡(β−γ)+cos⁡(γ−α)+cos⁡(α−β) is equal to
questions.solutionText: Solution Given, a = cos ⁡ α + i sin ⁡ α , b = cos ⁡ β + i sin ⁡ β , c = cos ⁡ γ + i sin ⁡ γ Using Euler's Formula ⟹ a = e i α , b = e i β , c = e i γ b c + c a + a b = e i ( β − γ ) + e i ( γ − α ) + e i ( α − β ) = 1 Taking real parts: cos ⁡ ( β − γ ) + cos ⁡ ( γ − α ) + cos ⁡ ( α − β ) = 1 Taking imaginary parts: sin ⁡ ( β − γ ) + sin ⁡ ( γ − α ) + sin ⁡ ( α − β ) = 0 (consistent) Correct option: (4) 1
questions.solution: Solution Given, a = cos ⁡ α + i sin ⁡ α , b = cos ⁡ β + i sin ⁡ β , c = cos ⁡ γ + i sin ⁡ γ Using Euler's Formula ⟹ a = e i α , b = e i β , c = e i γ b c + c a + a b = e i ( β − γ ) + e i ( γ − α ) + e i ( α − β ) = 1 Taking real parts: cos ⁡ ( β − γ ) + cos ⁡ ( γ − α ) + cos ⁡ ( α − β ) = 1 Taking imaginary parts: sin ⁡ ( β − γ ) + sin ⁡ ( γ − α ) + sin ⁡ ( α − β ) = 0 (consistent) Correct option: (4) 1
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given,
a=cos⁡α+isin⁡α,b=cos⁡β+isin⁡β, c=cos⁡γ+isin⁡γ

Using Euler's Formula
⟹a=eiα, b=eiβ, c=eiγ

bc+ca+ab=ei(β−γ)+ei(γ−α)+ei(α−β)=1

Taking real parts:
cos⁡(β−γ)+cos⁡(γ−α)+cos⁡(α−β)=1

Taking imaginary parts:
sin⁡(β−γ)+sin⁡(γ−α)+sin⁡(α−β)=0 (consistent)

Correct option: (4) 1

questions.text: λ = sin 2 ⁡ ( 5 o ) + sin 2 ⁡ ( 10 o ) + . . . . . . + sin 2 ⁡ ( 85 o ) . Then number of positive divisors of 2 λ – 8 is equal to
questions.questionText: λ = sin 2 ⁡ ( 5 o ) + sin 2 ⁡ ( 10 o ) + . . . . . . + sin 2 ⁡ ( 85 o ) . Then number of positive divisors of 2 λ – 8 is equal to
questions.question: λ = sin 2 ⁡ ( 5 o ) + sin 2 ⁡ ( 10 o ) + . . . . . . + sin 2 ⁡ ( 85 o ) . Then number of positive divisors of 2 λ – 8 is equal to
questions.prompt: λ = sin 2 ⁡ ( 5 o ) + sin 2 ⁡ ( 10 o ) + . . . . . . + sin 2 ⁡ ( 85 o ) . Then number of positive divisors of 2 λ – 8 is equal to
questions.html: λ =sin2⁡(5o)+sin2⁡(10o)+......+sin2⁡(85o). Then number of positive divisors of 2λ–8 is equal to
questions.questionHtml: λ =sin2⁡(5o)+sin2⁡(10o)+......+sin2⁡(85o). Then number of positive divisors of 2λ–8 is equal to
questions.solutionText: Solution λ = sin 2 ⁡ 5 ° + sin 2 ⁡ 10 ° + ⋯ + sin 2 ⁡ 85 ° Pair terms using sin 2 ⁡ θ + sin 2 ⁡ ( 90 ° − θ ) = sin 2 ⁡ θ + cos 2 ⁡ θ = 1 : Pairs: ( 5 ° , 85 ° ) , ( 10 ° , 80 ° ) , ( 15 ° , 75 ° ) , ( 20 ° , 70 ° ) , ( 25 ° , 65 ° ) , ( 30 ° , 60 ° ) , ( 35 ° , 55 ° ) , ( 40 ° , 50 ° ) → 8 pairs, each summing to 1. Middle term: sin 2 ⁡ 45 ° = 1 2 λ = 8 + 1 2 = 17 2 2 λ − 8 = 17 − 8 = 9 = 3 2 Number of positive divisors of 9 = 3 (i.e. 1, 3, 9) Correct option: (2) 3
questions.solution: Solution λ = sin 2 ⁡ 5 ° + sin 2 ⁡ 10 ° + ⋯ + sin 2 ⁡ 85 ° Pair terms using sin 2 ⁡ θ + sin 2 ⁡ ( 90 ° − θ ) = sin 2 ⁡ θ + cos 2 ⁡ θ = 1 : Pairs: ( 5 ° , 85 ° ) , ( 10 ° , 80 ° ) , ( 15 ° , 75 ° ) , ( 20 ° , 70 ° ) , ( 25 ° , 65 ° ) , ( 30 ° , 60 ° ) , ( 35 ° , 55 ° ) , ( 40 ° , 50 ° ) → 8 pairs, each summing to 1. Middle term: sin 2 ⁡ 45 ° = 1 2 λ = 8 + 1 2 = 17 2 2 λ − 8 = 17 − 8 = 9 = 3 2 Number of positive divisors of 9 = 3 (i.e. 1, 3, 9) Correct option: (2) 3
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
λ=sin2⁡5°+sin2⁡10°+⋯+sin2⁡85°

Pair terms using sin2⁡θ+sin2⁡(90°−θ)=sin2⁡θ+cos2⁡θ=1:

Pairs: (5°,85°), (10°,80°), (15°,75°), (20°,70°), (25°,65°), (30°,60°), (35°,55°), (40°,50°) → 8 pairs, each summing to 1.
Middle term: sin2⁡45°=12

λ=8+12=172

2λ−8=17−8=9=32

Number of positive divisors of 9=3 (i.e. 1, 3, 9)

Correct option: (2) 3

questions.text: The number of solutions for the equation sin 3 x cos x + sin 2 x cos 2 x + sin x cos 3 x = 1 in the interval 0 , 2 π is/are

questions.html: The number of solutions for the equation sin3xcosx+sin2xcos2x+sinxcos3x=1 in the interval 0,2π is/are
questions.questionHtml: The number of solutions for the equation sin3xcosx+sin2xcos2x+sinxcos3x=1 in the interval 0,2π is/are
questions.solutionText: Solution sin 3 x cos x + sin 2 x cos 2 x + sin x cos 3 x = 1 ⇒ sin x cos x sin 2 x + sin x cos x + cos 2 x = 1 ⇒ sin 2 x 2 1 + sin 2 x 2 = 1 ⇒ sin 2 x 2 + sin 2 x = 4 ⇒ sin 2 2 x + 2 sin 2 x - 4 = 0 ⇒ sin 2 x = - 2 ± 4 + 16 2 ⇒ sin 2 x = - 1 ± 5 This is not possible. S i n c e - 1 ≤ sin 2 x ≤ 1 Hence the given equation has no solution.
questions.solution: Solution sin 3 x cos x + sin 2 x cos 2 x + sin x cos 3 x = 1 ⇒ sin x cos x sin 2 x + sin x cos x + cos 2 x = 1 ⇒ sin 2 x 2 1 + sin 2 x 2 = 1 ⇒ sin 2 x 2 + sin 2 x = 4 ⇒ sin 2 2 x + 2 sin 2 x - 4 = 0 ⇒ sin 2 x = - 2 ± 4 + 16 2 ⇒ sin 2 x = - 1 ± 5 This is not possible. S i n c e - 1 ≤ sin 2 x ≤ 1 Hence the given equation has no solution.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

sin3xcosx+sin2xcos2x+sinxcos3x=1

⇒ sinxcosxsin2x+sinxcosx+cos2x=1

⇒sin2x21+sin2x2=1

⇒ sin2x2+sin 2x=4

⇒ sin22x+2sin2x-4=0

⇒ sin2x=-2±4+162

⇒ sin2x=-1±5

This is not possible. Since -1≤sin2x≤1

Hence the given equation has no solution.

questions.solutionText: Solution tan ⁡ θ + sec ⁡ θ = e x ⇒ 1 + sin ⁡ θ cos ⁡ θ = e x ⇒ cos ⁡ θ 2 + sin ⁡ θ 2 cos ⁡ θ 2 − sin ⁡ θ 2 = e x (Using componendo & Dividendo rule) ⇒ tan ⁡ θ 2 = e x − 1 e x + 1 as cos ⁡ θ = 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ 2 1 + tan 2 ⁡ θ 2 ⇒ cos ⁡ θ = 1 − ( e x − 1 e x + 1 ) 2 1 + ( e x − 1 e x + 1 ) 2 ⇒ cos ⁡ θ = 2 e x e 2 x + 1 ⇒ cos ⁡ θ = 2 e x + e − x
questions.solution: Solution tan ⁡ θ + sec ⁡ θ = e x ⇒ 1 + sin ⁡ θ cos ⁡ θ = e x ⇒ cos ⁡ θ 2 + sin ⁡ θ 2 cos ⁡ θ 2 − sin ⁡ θ 2 = e x (Using componendo & Dividendo rule) ⇒ tan ⁡ θ 2 = e x − 1 e x + 1 as cos ⁡ θ = 1 − tan 2 ⁡ θ 2 1 + tan 2 ⁡ θ 2 ⇒ cos ⁡ θ = 1 − ( e x − 1 e x + 1 ) 2 1 + ( e x − 1 e x + 1 ) 2 ⇒ cos ⁡ θ = 2 e x e 2 x + 1 ⇒ cos ⁡ θ = 2 e x + e − x
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
tan⁡θ+sec⁡θ=ex⇒1+sin⁡θcos⁡θ=ex⇒cos⁡θ2+sin⁡θ2cos⁡θ2−sin⁡θ2=ex
(Using componendo & Dividendo rule)
⇒tan⁡θ2=ex−1ex+1 as cos⁡θ=1−tan2⁡θ21+tan2⁡θ2⇒cos⁡θ=1−(ex−1ex+1)21+(ex−1ex+1)2⇒cos⁡θ=2exe2x+1⇒cos⁡θ=2ex+e−x

questions.question: The values of λ , for which ( λ , λ − 2 ) lies inside the ellipse 4 x 2 + 9 y 2 = 36 and outside the parabola y 2 = x , satisfy
questions.prompt: The values of λ , for which ( λ , λ − 2 ) lies inside the ellipse 4 x 2 + 9 y 2 = 36 and outside the parabola y 2 = x , satisfy
questions.html: The values of λ, for which (λ,λ−2) lies inside the ellipse 4x2+9y2=36 and outside the parabola y2=x, satisfy
questions.questionHtml: The values of λ, for which (λ,λ−2) lies inside the ellipse 4x2+9y2=36 and outside the parabola y2=x, satisfy
questions.solutionText: Solution The point ( λ , λ − 2 ) lies inside the ellipse 4 x 2 + 9 y 2 = 36 ⇒ 4 λ 2 + 9 ( λ − 2 ) 2 < 36 ⇒ 4 λ 2 + 9 λ 2 + 36 − 36 λ < 36 ⇒ 13 λ 2 − 36 λ < 0 ⇒ λ ( 13 λ − 36 ) < 0 ⇒ 0 < λ < 36 13 The point ( λ , λ − 2 ) also lies outside the parabola y 2 = x ( λ − 2 ) 2 − λ > 0 ⇒ λ 2 − 4 λ + 4 − λ > 0 ⇒ λ 2 − 5 λ + 4 > 0 ⇒ λ 2 − 4 λ − λ + 4 > 0 ⇒ ( λ − 4 ) ( λ − 1 ) > 0 ⇒ λ ∈ ( − ∞ , 1 ) ∪ ( 4 , ∞ ) From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we can conclude that 0 < λ < 1
questions.solution: Solution The point ( λ , λ − 2 ) lies inside the ellipse 4 x 2 + 9 y 2 = 36 ⇒ 4 λ 2 + 9 ( λ − 2 ) 2 < 36 ⇒ 4 λ 2 + 9 λ 2 + 36 − 36 λ < 36 ⇒ 13 λ 2 − 36 λ < 0 ⇒ λ ( 13 λ − 36 ) < 0 ⇒ 0 < λ < 36 13 The point ( λ , λ − 2 ) also lies outside the parabola y 2 = x ( λ − 2 ) 2 − λ > 0 ⇒ λ 2 − 4 λ + 4 − λ > 0 ⇒ λ 2 − 5 λ + 4 > 0 ⇒ λ 2 − 4 λ − λ + 4 > 0 ⇒ ( λ − 4 ) ( λ − 1 ) > 0 ⇒ λ ∈ ( − ∞ , 1 ) ∪ ( 4 , ∞ ) From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we can conclude that 0 < λ < 1
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The point (λ,λ−2) lies inside the ellipse

4x2+9y2=36⇒4λ2+9(λ−2)2<36⇒4λ2+9λ2+36−36λ<36⇒13λ2−36λ<0⇒λ(13λ−36)<0⇒0<λ<3613

The point (λ,λ−2) also lies outside the parabola y2=x

(λ−2)2−λ>0⇒λ2−4λ+4−λ>0⇒λ2−5λ+4>0⇒λ2−4λ−λ+4>0⇒(λ−4)(λ−1)>0⇒λ∈(−∞,1)∪(4,∞)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we can conclude that 0<λ<1

questions.html: The differential equation of the family of parabolas with focus at the origin and the x-axis as axis is
questions.questionHtml: The differential equation of the family of parabolas with focus at the origin and the x-axis as axis is
questions.solutionText: Solution Equation of family of parabolas with focus at ( 0 , 0 ) and x -axis as axis is y 2 = 4 a ( x + a ) ...(i) Differentiating (i) with respect to x , 2 y y 1 = 4 a , y 2 = 2 y y 1 ( x + y y 1 2 ) y = 2 x y 1 + y y 1 2 ⇒ y ( d y d x ) 2 + 2 x d y d x = y .
questions.solution: Solution Equation of family of parabolas with focus at ( 0 , 0 ) and x -axis as axis is y 2 = 4 a ( x + a ) ...(i) Differentiating (i) with respect to x , 2 y y 1 = 4 a , y 2 = 2 y y 1 ( x + y y 1 2 ) y = 2 x y 1 + y y 1 2 ⇒ y ( d y d x ) 2 + 2 x d y d x = y .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Equation of family of parabolas with focus at (0,0) and x-axis as axis is y2=4a(x+a) ...(i)

Differentiating (i) with respect to x,

2yy1=4a,y2=2yy1(x+yy12)y=2xy1+yy12⇒y(dydx)2+2xdydx=y.

questions.text: If f ( x ) = cos ⁡ ( tan − 1 ⁡ ( sin ⁡ ( cot − 1 ⁡ x ) ) ) , which of the following expressions represents the simplified form of f ( x ) for all x ∈ R ?
questions.questionText: If f ( x ) = cos ⁡ ( tan − 1 ⁡ ( sin ⁡ ( cot − 1 ⁡ x ) ) ) , which of the following expressions represents the simplified form of f ( x ) for all x ∈ R ?
questions.question: If f ( x ) = cos ⁡ ( tan − 1 ⁡ ( sin ⁡ ( cot − 1 ⁡ x ) ) ) , which of the following expressions represents the simplified form of f ( x ) for all x ∈ R ?
questions.prompt: If f ( x ) = cos ⁡ ( tan − 1 ⁡ ( sin ⁡ ( cot − 1 ⁡ x ) ) ) , which of the following expressions represents the simplified form of f ( x ) for all x ∈ R ?
questions.html: If f(x)=cos⁡(tan−1⁡(sin⁡(cot−1⁡x))), which of the following expressions represents the simplified form of f(x) for all x∈R ?
questions.questionHtml: If f(x)=cos⁡(tan−1⁡(sin⁡(cot−1⁡x))), which of the following expressions represents the simplified form of f(x) for all x∈R ?
questions.solutionText: Solution cot − 1 ⁡ x = θ ⇒ sin ⁡ θ = 1 x 2 + 1 ⇒ sin ⁡ ( cot − 1 ⁡ x ) = 1 x 2 + 1 Let tan − 1 ⁡ ( 1 x 2 + 1 ) = ϕ ⇒ tan ⁡ ϕ = 1 x 2 + 1 cos ⁡ ϕ = x 2 + 1 x 2 + 2 f ( x ) = cos ⁡ ϕ = x 2 + 1 x 2 + 2 Correct option: (B)
questions.solution: Solution cot − 1 ⁡ x = θ ⇒ sin ⁡ θ = 1 x 2 + 1 ⇒ sin ⁡ ( cot − 1 ⁡ x ) = 1 x 2 + 1 Let tan − 1 ⁡ ( 1 x 2 + 1 ) = ϕ ⇒ tan ⁡ ϕ = 1 x 2 + 1 cos ⁡ ϕ = x 2 + 1 x 2 + 2 f ( x ) = cos ⁡ ϕ = x 2 + 1 x 2 + 2 Correct option: (B)
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
cot−1⁡x=θ ⇒ sin⁡θ=1x2+1

⇒ sin⁡(cot−1⁡x)=1x2+1

Let tan−1⁡(1x2+1)=ϕ ⇒ tan⁡ϕ=1x2+1

cos⁡ϕ=x2+1x2+2

f(x)=cos⁡ϕ=x2+1x2+2

Correct option: (B)

questions.prompt: ∫ sin ⁡ 2 x sin 4 ⁡ x + cos 4 ⁡ x d x =
questions.html: ∫sin⁡2xsin4⁡x+cos4⁡xdx=
questions.questionHtml: ∫sin⁡2xsin4⁡x+cos4⁡xdx=
questions.solutionText: Solution I = ∫ sin ⁡ 2 x sin 4 ⁡ x + cos 4 ⁡ x d x Divide numerator and denominator by cos 4 ⁡ x : I = ∫ sin ⁡ 2 x / cos 4 ⁡ x tan 4 ⁡ x + 1 d x = ∫ 2 sin ⁡ x cos ⁡ x / cos 4 ⁡ x tan 4 ⁡ x + 1 d x = ∫ 2 tan ⁡ x sec 2 ⁡ x 1 + tan 4 ⁡ x d x Let t = tan 2 ⁡ x ⇒ d t = 2 tan ⁡ x sec 2 ⁡ x d x I = ∫ d t 1 + t 2 = tan − 1 ⁡ ( t ) + c = tan − 1 ⁡ ( tan 2 ⁡ x ) + c Correct option: (2)
questions.solution: Solution I = ∫ sin ⁡ 2 x sin 4 ⁡ x + cos 4 ⁡ x d x Divide numerator and denominator by cos 4 ⁡ x : I = ∫ sin ⁡ 2 x / cos 4 ⁡ x tan 4 ⁡ x + 1 d x = ∫ 2 sin ⁡ x cos ⁡ x / cos 4 ⁡ x tan 4 ⁡ x + 1 d x = ∫ 2 tan ⁡ x sec 2 ⁡ x 1 + tan 4 ⁡ x d x Let t = tan 2 ⁡ x ⇒ d t = 2 tan ⁡ x sec 2 ⁡ x d x I = ∫ d t 1 + t 2 = tan − 1 ⁡ ( t ) + c = tan − 1 ⁡ ( tan 2 ⁡ x ) + c Correct option: (2)
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
I=∫sin⁡2xsin4⁡x+cos4⁡xdx

Divide numerator and denominator by cos4⁡x:
I=∫sin⁡2x/cos4⁡xtan4⁡x+1dx=∫2sin⁡xcos⁡x/cos4⁡xtan4⁡x+1dx=∫2tan⁡xsec2⁡x1+tan4⁡xdx

Let t=tan2⁡x ⇒ dt=2tan⁡xsec2⁡x dx

I=∫dt1+t2=tan−1⁡(t)+c=tan−1⁡(tan2⁡x)+c

Correct option: (2)

questions.html:
Consider the following propositions:

p : I have a pen

q : I can write questions.

Then the statement of the proposition ‘‘If I have a pen, then I can write questions’’ is equivalent to
questions.questionHtml:
Consider the following propositions:

p : I have a pen

q : I can write questions.

Then the statement of the proposition ‘‘If I have a pen, then I can write questions’’ is equivalent to
questions.solutionText: Solution The statement is of the form if p then q , which is a conditional proposition represented as p → q . p ∨ q → p or q (disjunction) p ∧ q → p and q (conjunction) p ↔ q → p if and only if q (biconditional) p → q → if p then q (conditional) Correct option: (1) p → q
questions.solution: Solution The statement is of the form if p then q , which is a conditional proposition represented as p → q . p ∨ q → p or q (disjunction) p ∧ q → p and q (conjunction) p ↔ q → p if and only if q (biconditional) p → q → if p then q (conditional) Correct option: (1) p → q
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The statement is of the form if p then q, which is a conditional proposition represented as p→q.

p∨q → p or q (disjunction)
p∧q → p and q (conjunction)
p↔q → p if and only if q (biconditional)
p→q → if p then q (conditional)

Correct option: (1) p→q

questions.questionHtml: If Ai=[aibibiai] and if |a|<1,|b|<1, then ∑i=1∞det(Ai) is equal to
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
det(Ai)=a2i−b2i

∑i=1∞det(Ai)=∑i=1∞(a2i−b2i)=∑i=1∞a2i−∑i=1∞b2i

=a21−a2−b21−b2

=a2(1−b2)−b2(1−a2)(1−a2)(1−b2)

=a2−a2b2−b2+a2b2(1−a2)(1−b2)

=a2−b2(1−a2)(1−b2)

Correct option: (2)

questions.solutionText: Solution ∫ e − x ( 1 − tan ⁡ x ) sec ⁡ x d x = ∫ e − x ( sec ⁡ x − sec ⁡ x tan ⁡ x ) d x Use the formula ∫ e − x [ f ( x ) − f ′ ( x ) ] d x = − e − x f ( x ) + C Here f ( x ) = sec ⁡ x , f ′ ( x ) = sec ⁡ x tan ⁡ x → matches the form. = − e − x sec ⁡ x + C Correct option: (4)
questions.solution: Solution ∫ e − x ( 1 − tan ⁡ x ) sec ⁡ x d x = ∫ e − x ( sec ⁡ x − sec ⁡ x tan ⁡ x ) d x Use the formula ∫ e − x [ f ( x ) − f ′ ( x ) ] d x = − e − x f ( x ) + C Here f ( x ) = sec ⁡ x , f ′ ( x ) = sec ⁡ x tan ⁡ x → matches the form. = − e − x sec ⁡ x + C Correct option: (4)
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
∫e−x(1−tan⁡x)sec⁡x dx=∫e−x(sec⁡x−sec⁡xtan⁡x) dx

Use the formula ∫e−x[f(x)−f′(x)]dx=−e−xf(x)+C

Here f(x)=sec⁡x, f′(x)=sec⁡xtan⁡x → matches the form.

=−e−xsec⁡x+C

Correct option: (4)

questions.text: The trace T r ( A ) of a 3 × 3 matrix A = ( a j j ) is defined by the relation T r ( A ) = a 11 + a 22 + a 33 (i.e, T r ( A ) is sum of the main diagonal elements). Which of the following is NOT true in general?
questions.questionText: The trace T r ( A ) of a 3 × 3 matrix A = ( a j j ) is defined by the relation T r ( A ) = a 11 + a 22 + a 33 (i.e, T r ( A ) is sum of the main diagonal elements). Which of the following is NOT true in general?
questions.question: The trace T r ( A ) of a 3 × 3 matrix A = ( a j j ) is defined by the relation T r ( A ) = a 11 + a 22 + a 33 (i.e, T r ( A ) is sum of the main diagonal elements). Which of the following is NOT true in general?
questions.prompt: The trace T r ( A ) of a 3 × 3 matrix A = ( a j j ) is defined by the relation T r ( A ) = a 11 + a 22 + a 33 (i.e, T r ( A ) is sum of the main diagonal elements). Which of the following is NOT true in general?
questions.html: The trace Tr(A) of a 3×3 matrix A=(ajj) is defined by the relation Tr(A)=a11+a22+a33 (i.e, Tr(A) is sum of the main diagonal elements). Which of the following is NOT true in general?
questions.questionHtml: The trace Tr(A) of a 3×3 matrix A=(ajj) is defined by the relation Tr(A)=a11+a22+a33 (i.e, Tr(A) is sum of the main diagonal elements). Which of the following is NOT true in general?
questions.solutionText: Solution (1) T r ( k A ) = k T r ( A ) → true, since each diagonal element scales by k . (2) T r ( A + B ) = T r ( A ) + T r ( B ) → true, by linearity of diagonal sum. (3) T r ( I 3 ) = 1 + 1 + 1 = 3 → true. (4) T r ( A 2 ) = ( T r ( A ) ) 2 → NOT true in general. Counterexample: A = ( 1 1 0 1 ) , T r ( A ) = 2 , ( T r ( A ) ) 2 = 4 But A 2 = ( 1 2 0 1 ) , T r ( A 2 ) = 2 ≠ 4 Correct option: (4)
questions.solution: Solution (1) T r ( k A ) = k T r ( A ) → true, since each diagonal element scales by k . (2) T r ( A + B ) = T r ( A ) + T r ( B ) → true, by linearity of diagonal sum. (3) T r ( I 3 ) = 1 + 1 + 1 = 3 → true. (4) T r ( A 2 ) = ( T r ( A ) ) 2 → NOT true in general. Counterexample: A = ( 1 1 0 1 ) , T r ( A ) = 2 , ( T r ( A ) ) 2 = 4 But A 2 = ( 1 2 0 1 ) , T r ( A 2 ) = 2 ≠ 4 Correct option: (4)
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
(1) Tr(kA)=kTr(A) → true, since each diagonal element scales by k.

(2) Tr(A+B)=Tr(A)+Tr(B) → true, by linearity of diagonal sum.

(3) Tr(I3)=1+1+1=3 → true.

(4) Tr(A2)=(Tr(A))2 → NOT true in general.
Counterexample: A=(1101), Tr(A)=2, (Tr(A))2=4
But A2=(1201), Tr(A2)=2≠4

Correct option: (4)

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The difference between any two consecutive interior angles of a polygon is 5 o . If the smallest angle is 120 o , then the number of the sides of the polygon is
questions.questionText: The difference between any two consecutive interior angles of a polygon is 5 o . If the smallest angle is 120 o , then the number of the sides of the polygon is
questions.question: The difference between any two consecutive interior angles of a polygon is 5 o . If the smallest angle is 120 o , then the number of the sides of the polygon is
questions.prompt: The difference between any two consecutive interior angles of a polygon is 5 o . If the smallest angle is 120 o , then the number of the sides of the polygon is
questions.html: The difference between any two consecutive interior angles of a polygon is 5o. If the smallest angle is 120o, then the number of the sides of the polygon is
questions.questionHtml: The difference between any two consecutive interior angles of a polygon is 5o. If the smallest angle is 120o, then the number of the sides of the polygon is
questions.solutionText: Solution Here the angles of a polygon of n sides form an A . P . whose first term is 120 o and common difference is 5 o . Now, the sum of interior angles S n = n 2 [ 2 a + ( n − 1 ) d ] = n 2 [ 2 × 120 + ( n − 1 ) 5 ] S n = n 2 [ 240 + 5 n − 5 ] = n 2 ( 235 + 5 n ) ...(1) Also the sum of interior angles = 180 ( n − 2 ) From equation (1), ∴ n 2 235 + 5 n = 180 n - 360 ⇒ 5 n 2 ( 47 + n ) = 180 ( n − 2 ) ⇒ n ( 47 + n ) = 72 ( n −2) ⇒ n 2 + 47 n = 72 n − 144 ⇒ n 2 - 25 n + 144 = 0 ⇒ ( n - 16 ) ( n - 9 ) = 0 ⇒ n ≠ 16 ∴ n = 9 . ∴ n ≠ 16 , because for n = 16 , one of the interior angle will become 180 o , which is not possible. For n = 16 ⇒ 120 o , 125 o , ....... , 180 o , ..... , 195 o and for n = 9 ⇒ 120 o , 125 o , ...... , 160 o .
questions.solution: Solution Here the angles of a polygon of n sides form an A . P . whose first term is 120 o and common difference is 5 o . Now, the sum of interior angles S n = n 2 [ 2 a + ( n − 1 ) d ] = n 2 [ 2 × 120 + ( n − 1 ) 5 ] S n = n 2 [ 240 + 5 n − 5 ] = n 2 ( 235 + 5 n ) ...(1) Also the sum of interior angles = 180 ( n − 2 ) From equation (1), ∴ n 2 235 + 5 n = 180 n - 360 ⇒ 5 n 2 ( 47 + n ) = 180 ( n − 2 ) ⇒ n ( 47 + n ) = 72 ( n −2) ⇒ n 2 + 47 n = 72 n − 144 ⇒ n 2 - 25 n + 144 = 0 ⇒ ( n - 16 ) ( n - 9 ) = 0 ⇒ n ≠ 16 ∴ n = 9 . ∴ n ≠ 16 , because for n = 16 , one of the interior angle will become 180 o , which is not possible. For n = 16 ⇒ 120 o , 125 o , ....... , 180 o , ..... , 195 o and for n = 9 ⇒ 120 o , 125 o , ...... , 160 o .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Here the angles of a polygon of n sides form an A.P. whose first term is 120o and common difference is 5o.
Now, the sum of interior angles
Sn=n2[2a+(n−1)d]=n2[2×120+(n−1)5]
Sn=n2[240+5n−5]=n2(235+5n) ...(1)
Also the sum of interior angles =180(n−2)
From equation (1),
∴n2235+5n=180n-360
⇒5n2(47+n)=180(n−2)
⇒n(47+n)=72(n−2)
⇒n2+47n=72n−144
⇒n2-25n+144=0
⇒(n-16)(n-9)=0

⇒n≠16∴ n=9.
∴ n≠16, because for n=16, one of the interior angle will become 180o, which is not possible.
For n=16⇒120o, 125o,.......,180o,.....,195o and forn=9⇒120o, 125o, ......,160o.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The mean monthly salary of the employees in a certain factory is Rs. 500. The mean monthly salaries of male and female employees are respectively Rs. 510 and Rs. 460. The percentage of male employees in the factory is
questions.questionText: The mean monthly salary of the employees in a certain factory is Rs. 500. The mean monthly salaries of male and female employees are respectively Rs. 510 and Rs. 460. The percentage of male employees in the factory is
questions.question: The mean monthly salary of the employees in a certain factory is Rs. 500. The mean monthly salaries of male and female employees are respectively Rs. 510 and Rs. 460. The percentage of male employees in the factory is
questions.prompt: The mean monthly salary of the employees in a certain factory is Rs. 500. The mean monthly salaries of male and female employees are respectively Rs. 510 and Rs. 460. The percentage of male employees in the factory is
questions.html: The mean monthly salary of the employees in a certain factory is Rs. 500. The mean monthly salaries of male and female employees are respectively Rs. 510 and Rs. 460. The percentage of male employees in the factory is
questions.questionHtml: The mean monthly salary of the employees in a certain factory is Rs. 500. The mean monthly salaries of male and female employees are respectively Rs. 510 and Rs. 460. The percentage of male employees in the factory is
questions.solutionText: Solution The formula for combined mean is x ¯ = n 1 x ¯ 1 + n 2 x ¯ 2 n 1 + n 2 Given, x ¯ = 500 , x ¯ 1 = 510 , x ¯ 2 = 460 Let n 1 + n 2 = 100 and n 1 denotes male, n 2 denotes female for this n 2 = 100 − n 1 500 = 510 n 1 + ( 100 − n 1 ) 460 100 ⇒ 50000 = 510 n 1 + 46000 − 460 n 1 ⇒ 50000 − 46000 = 50 n 1 ⇒ 4000 = 50 n 1 ⇒ n 1 = 4000 50 = 80 .
questions.solution: Solution The formula for combined mean is x ¯ = n 1 x ¯ 1 + n 2 x ¯ 2 n 1 + n 2 Given, x ¯ = 500 , x ¯ 1 = 510 , x ¯ 2 = 460 Let n 1 + n 2 = 100 and n 1 denotes male, n 2 denotes female for this n 2 = 100 − n 1 500 = 510 n 1 + ( 100 − n 1 ) 460 100 ⇒ 50000 = 510 n 1 + 46000 − 460 n 1 ⇒ 50000 − 46000 = 50 n 1 ⇒ 4000 = 50 n 1 ⇒ n 1 = 4000 50 = 80 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The formula for combined mean is
x¯=n1x¯1+n2x¯2n1+n2
Given, x¯=500,x¯1=510,x¯2=460
Let n1+n2=100 and n1 denotes male, n2 denotes female for this n2=100−n1
500=510n1+(100−n1)460100⇒50000=510n1+46000−460n1⇒50000−46000=50n1⇒4000=50n1⇒n1=400050=80.

questions.solutionText: Solution y = x a x 2 + b For maximum, d y d x = 0 : d y d x = ( a x 2 + b ) − x ( 2 a x ) ( a x 2 + b ) 2 = b − a x 2 ( a x 2 + b ) 2 = 0 ⇒ x = b a Maximum value: y = b / a a ⋅ ( b / a ) + b = b / a b + b = b / a 2 b = 1 2 b ⋅ b a = 1 2 a b Correct option: (2) 1 2 a b

y=xax2+b

For maximum, dydx=0:

questions.text: The range of the function f ( x ) = sin − 1 x + cos − 1 x − tan − 1 x , is _____
questions.questionText: The range of the function f ( x ) = sin − 1 x + cos − 1 x − tan − 1 x , is _____
questions.question: The range of the function f ( x ) = sin − 1 x + cos − 1 x − tan − 1 x , is _____
questions.prompt: The range of the function f ( x ) = sin − 1 x + cos − 1 x − tan − 1 x , is _____
questions.html: The range of the function f(x) = sin−1 x + cos−1 x − tan−1 x, is _____
questions.questionHtml: The range of the function f(x) = sin−1 x + cos−1 x − tan−1 x, is _____
questions.solutionText: Solution Domain of f ( x ) : sin − 1 ⁡ x and cos − 1 ⁡ x require x ∈ [ − 1 , 1 ] , and tan − 1 ⁡ x is defined for all x . So domain = [ − 1 , 1 ] . Use sin − 1 ⁡ x + cos − 1 ⁡ x = π 2 : f ( x ) = π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ x For x ∈ [ − 1 , 1 ] : tan − 1 ⁡ x ∈ [ − π 4 , π 4 ] So π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ x ∈ [ π 2 − π 4 , π 2 + π 4 ] = [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ] f ( x ) = π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ x ∈ [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ] = [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ] Correct option: (4) [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ]
questions.solution: Solution Domain of f ( x ) : sin − 1 ⁡ x and cos − 1 ⁡ x require x ∈ [ − 1 , 1 ] , and tan − 1 ⁡ x is defined for all x . So domain = [ − 1 , 1 ] . Use sin − 1 ⁡ x + cos − 1 ⁡ x = π 2 : f ( x ) = π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ x For x ∈ [ − 1 , 1 ] : tan − 1 ⁡ x ∈ [ − π 4 , π 4 ] So π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ x ∈ [ π 2 − π 4 , π 2 + π 4 ] = [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ] f ( x ) = π 2 − tan − 1 ⁡ x ∈ [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ] = [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ] Correct option: (4) [ π 4 , 3 π 4 ]
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Domain of f(x): sin−1⁡x and cos−1⁡x require x∈[−1,1], and tan−1⁡x is defined for all x. So domain =[−1,1].

Use sin−1⁡x+cos−1⁡x=π2:

f(x)=π2−tan−1⁡x

For x∈[−1,1]: tan−1⁡x∈[−π4,π4]

So π2−tan−1⁡x∈[π2−π4, π2+π4]=[π4,3π4]

f(x)=π2−tan−1⁡x∈[π4, 3π4]=[π4, 3π4]

Correct option: (4) [π4, 3π4]

questions.solutionText: Solution 10 girls and 8 boys (Select 8 girls and 6 boys), Mr. Ravi refuses to speak, if Ms. Rani is a speaker. Ms. Rani refuses to speak, if Ms. Radha is a speaker. Let A → Mr. Ravi is a speaker B → Ms. Rani is a speaker C → Ms. Radha is a speaker Set satisfying the given condition is represented in the Venn diagram = n U - n A ∩ B - n B ∩ C + n A ∩ B ∩ C Number of ways of preparing the list = C 8 10 . C 6 8 - C 7 9 . C 5 7 - C 6 8 . C 6 8 + C 5 7 . C 6 8 = 45 × 28 - 36 × 21 - 28 × 28 + 28 × 21 = 308

questions.html: If a=cos⁡2π7+isin⁡2π7, then the quadratic equation whose roots are α=a+a2+a4 and β=a3+a5+a6 , is
questions.questionHtml: If a=cos⁡2π7+isin⁡2π7, then the quadratic equation whose roots are α=a+a2+a4 and β=a3+a5+a6 , is
questions.solutionText: Solution Given, a = cos ⁡ 2 π 7 + i . sin ⁡ 2 π 7 ∴ a 7 = cos ⁡ 2 π + i sin ⁡ 2 π ∵ e i θ = cos ⁡ θ + i sin ⁡ θ = 1 Also, α = a + a 2 + a 4 , β = a 3 + a 5 + a 6 Then the sum of roots, S = α + β = a + a 2 + a 3 + a 4 + a 5 + a 6 ⇒ S = a 1 - a 6 1 - a = a - a 7 1 - a = a - 1 1 - a = - 1 ∵ a 7 = 1 Product of the roots, P = α β = a + a 2 + a 4 a 3 + a 5 + a 6 = a 4 + a 5 + 1 + a 6 + 1 + a 2 + 1 + a + a 3 ∵ a 7 = 1 = 3 + a + a 2 + a 3 + a 4 + a 5 + a 6 = 3 - 1 = 2 Hence, the required quadratic equation is x 2 + x + 2 = 0
questions.solution: Solution Given, a = cos ⁡ 2 π 7 + i . sin ⁡ 2 π 7 ∴ a 7 = cos ⁡ 2 π + i sin ⁡ 2 π ∵ e i θ = cos ⁡ θ + i sin ⁡ θ = 1 Also, α = a + a 2 + a 4 , β = a 3 + a 5 + a 6 Then the sum of roots, S = α + β = a + a 2 + a 3 + a 4 + a 5 + a 6 ⇒ S = a 1 - a 6 1 - a = a - a 7 1 - a = a - 1 1 - a = - 1 ∵ a 7 = 1 Product of the roots, P = α β = a + a 2 + a 4 a 3 + a 5 + a 6 = a 4 + a 5 + 1 + a 6 + 1 + a 2 + 1 + a + a 3 ∵ a 7 = 1 = 3 + a + a 2 + a 3 + a 4 + a 5 + a 6 = 3 - 1 = 2 Hence, the required quadratic equation is x 2 + x + 2 = 0
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given, a=cos⁡2π7+i.sin⁡2π7

∴ a7=cos⁡2π+isin⁡2π ∵ eiθ=cos⁡θ+isin⁡θ

=1

Also, α=a+a2+a4, β=a3+a5+a6

Then the sum of roots,

S=α+β=a+a2+a3+a4+a5+a6

⇒ S=a1-a61-a=a-a71-a

=a-11-a=-1 ∵a7=1

Product of the roots,

P=αβ=a+a2+a4 a3+a5+a6

=a4+a5+1+a6+1+a2+1+a+a3 ∵ a7=1

=3+a+a2+a3+a4+a5+a6=3-1=2

Hence, the required quadratic equation is x2+x+2=0

questions.text: The objective function Z = 4 x + 3 y is to be maximized subject to the following constraints: x + y ≤ 4 3 x + y ≤ 6 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 What is the maximum value of Z ?

questions.prompt: The objective function Z = 4 x + 3 y is to be maximized subject to the following constraints: x + y ≤ 4 3 x + y ≤ 6 x ≥ 0 , y ≥ 0 What is the maximum value of Z ?

The objective function Z=4x+3y is to be maximized subject to the following constraints:
x+y≤4
3x+y≤6
x≥0,y≥0

questions.questionHtml:
The objective function Z=4x+3y is to be maximized subject to the following constraints:
x+y≤4
3x+y≤6
x≥0,y≥0

What is the maximum value of Z ?
questions.solutionText: Solution Corner points of the feasible region: ( 0 , 0 ) : Z = 0 ( 2 , 0 ) : from 3 x + y = 6 at y = 0 ⇒ x = 2 : Z = 8 ( 0 , 4 ) : from x + y = 4 at x = 0 : Z = 12 Intersection of x + y = 4 and 3 x + y = 6 : Subtracting: 2 x = 2 ⇒ x = 1 , y = 3 ( 1 , 3 ) : Z = 4 + 9 = 13 Maximum Z = 13 at ( 1 , 3 ) Correct option: C) 13
questions.solution: Solution Corner points of the feasible region: ( 0 , 0 ) : Z = 0 ( 2 , 0 ) : from 3 x + y = 6 at y = 0 ⇒ x = 2 : Z = 8 ( 0 , 4 ) : from x + y = 4 at x = 0 : Z = 12 Intersection of x + y = 4 and 3 x + y = 6 : Subtracting: 2 x = 2 ⇒ x = 1 , y = 3 ( 1 , 3 ) : Z = 4 + 9 = 13 Maximum Z = 13 at ( 1 , 3 ) Correct option: C) 13
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Corner points of the feasible region:

(0,0): Z=0
(2,0): from 3x+y=6 at y=0 ⇒ x=2: Z=8
(0,4): from x+y=4 at x=0: Z=12

Intersection of x+y=4 and 3x+y=6:
Subtracting: 2x=2 ⇒ x=1, y=3
(1,3): Z=4+9=13

Maximum Z=13 at (1,3)

Correct option: C) 13

questions.questionHtml: In the set of all 3×3 real matrices a relation is defined as follows. A matrix A is related to a matrix B, if and only if there is a non-singular 3×3 matrix P, such that B=P−1AP. This relation is
questions.solutionText: Solution Let the relation defined as R = { ( A , B ) ∣ B = P − 1 A P } For reflexive, A = I − 1 A l ⇒ ( A , A ) ∈ R ⇒ R is reflexive For symmetric Let ( A B ) ∈ R ∵ B = P − 1 A P ⇒ P B = A P ⇒ P B P − 1 = A ⇒ A = ( P − 1 ) − 1 B ( P − 1 ) ⇒ ( B , A ) ∈ R ⇒ R is symmetric. For transitive Let ( A , B ) ∈ R , ( B , C ) ∈ R ∵ A = P − 1 B P and B = Q − 1 C Q ⇒ A = P − Q − 1 C Q P = ( Q P ) − 1 C ( Q P ) ⇒ ( A , C ) ∈ R ⇒ R is transitive. since, A is reflexive, symmetric and transitive. So, R is an equivalence relation.
questions.solution: Solution Let the relation defined as R = { ( A , B ) ∣ B = P − 1 A P } For reflexive, A = I − 1 A l ⇒ ( A , A ) ∈ R ⇒ R is reflexive For symmetric Let ( A B ) ∈ R ∵ B = P − 1 A P ⇒ P B = A P ⇒ P B P − 1 = A ⇒ A = ( P − 1 ) − 1 B ( P − 1 ) ⇒ ( B , A ) ∈ R ⇒ R is symmetric. For transitive Let ( A , B ) ∈ R , ( B , C ) ∈ R ∵ A = P − 1 B P and B = Q − 1 C Q ⇒ A = P − Q − 1 C Q P = ( Q P ) − 1 C ( Q P ) ⇒ ( A , C ) ∈ R ⇒ R is transitive. since, A is reflexive, symmetric and transitive. So, R is an equivalence relation.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let the relation defined as R={(A,B)∣B=P−1AP}
For reflexive, A=I−1Al
⇒(A,A)∈R
⇒R is reflexive
For symmetric Let(AB)∈R
∵B=P−1AP
⇒PB=AP⇒PBP−1=A
⇒A=(P−1)−1B(P−1)
⇒(B,A)∈R⇒R is symmetric.
For transitive
Let (A,B)∈R,(B,C)∈R
∵A=P−1BP and B=Q−1CQ
⇒A=P−Q−1CQP=(QP)−1C(QP)
⇒(A,C)∈R
⇒R is transitive. since, A is reflexive, symmetric and transitive. So, R is an equivalence relation.
